title: Fixes in Imagenet training script
body:
* ngpus_per_node variable count fix
* dist_backend assertion added for nccl issue

step_plan.summary: Fixes distributed ImageNet training startup by correcting the per-node GPU count used for spawning/scaling and by adding a fail-fast assertion to prevent invalid NCCL backend usage (e.g., NCCL without CUDA), reducing distributed initialization failures.

steps.operation: Fix `ngpus_per_node` computation and ensure it is used consistently for world-size scaling and process spawning
steps.reason: `ngpus_per_node` drives `mp.spawn(..., nprocs=ngpus_per_node)`, rank calculation in workers, and `world_size` scaling in multiprocessing-distributed mode. If it is computed incorrectly (or from the wrong source), it causes incorrect number of spawned processes, wrong `args.world_size`, and downstream batch/workers scaling errors.
steps.side_effects: Changes behavior of distributed startup: number of processes spawned per node and computed `world_size` may differ from current buggy behavior. May expose misconfigurations (e.g., expecting CPU-only distributed with GPU count logic). Ensure distributed launch commands that previously “worked by accident” are updated/validated.

steps.operation: Add a fail-fast assertion/validation for `dist_backend` vs device availability (NCCL guard) before initializing process groups
steps.reason: NCCL requires CUDA and is the expected backend for multi-GPU multiprocessing distributed training. Adding an explicit assertion before `dist.init_process_group(...)` prevents hard-to-debug NCCL initialization failures when CUDA is unavailable or when an invalid backend/device combination is selected.
steps.side_effects: Introduces stricter runtime checks that will raise errors earlier for invalid configurations (e.g., `--dist-backend nccl` on CPU-only nodes). This may require users to switch to `gloo` for CPU distributed runs or enable CUDA properly; also may affect CI environments that lack GPUs.
